title: Goroutines, Channels, Select, Ownership, and Pipelines · Go Semantics Case: Channel Range State
summary: A report pipeline must process bounded work concurrently, preserve result ownership, close channels correctly, and terminate every goroutine. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go channel range state increment in a bounded concurrent report pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish open, closed, empty, nil, and buffered channel states and interpret two-result receives and range termination.
Use unbuffered, buffered, and directional channels while assigning close authority to the sender that knows no values remain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	results := make(chan int, 1)
	go func() { results <- 5 * 5; close(results) }()
	value, ok := <-results
	fmt.Println(value, ok)
}
content.caption: Runnable Go value, failure, or concurrency contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
